channel.title: NHC Eastern North Pacific
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Eastern North Pacific

item.title: Eastern North Pacific Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABPZ20 KNHC 220517
TWOEP

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
1100 PM PDT Tue Jul 21 2026

For the eastern and central North Pacific east of 180 longitude:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Hurricane
Fausto, located several hundred miles west-southwest of the southern
tip of the Baja California Peninsula.

Eastern and Central East Pacific:
A tropical wave located a few hundred miles off the coast of Central
America is producing a large area of disorganized showers and
thunderstorms. Environmental conditions appear conducive for
gradual development of this system, and a tropical depression is
expected to form over the weekend while it moves west-northwestward
offshore of Mexico.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...20 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...high...90 percent.

Well Southwest of Hawaii:
A trough of low pressure located well southwest of the Hawaiian
Islands is producing disorganized showers and thunderstorms.
Environmental conditions appear conducive for some gradual
development of this system as it moves slowly westward to
west-northwestward during the next several days, and a tropical
depression could form over the weekend as the system approaches the
International Date Line.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...near 0 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...medium...50 percent.

$$
Forecaster Kelly
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Hurricane Fausto (EP1/EP062026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...FAUSTO STRENGTHENS A LITTLE MORE... As of 11:00 PM HST Tue Jul 21 the center of Fausto was located near 16.7, -120.7 with movement WNW at 8 mph. The minimum central pressure was 981 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 85 mph.

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Public Advisory Number 14
item.description:
Issued at 1100 PM HST Tue Jul 21 2026 
000
WTPZ31 KNHC 220834
TCPEP1
 
BULLETIN
Hurricane Fausto Advisory Number  14
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
1100 PM HST Tue Jul 21 2026
 
...FAUSTO STRENGTHENS A LITTLE MORE...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 1100 PM HST...0900 UTC...INFORMATION
-----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...16.7N 120.7W
ABOUT 820 MI...1320 KM WSW OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...85 MPH...140 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...WNW OR 285 DEGREES AT 8 MPH...13 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...981 MB...28.97 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 1100 PM HST (0900 UTC), the center of Hurricane Fausto was 
located near latitude 16.7 North, longitude 120.7 West. Fausto is 
moving toward the west-northwest near 8 mph (13 km/h). A general 
west-northwestward to westward motion, with a slight increase in 
forward speed is expected for the next several days.
 
Maximum sustained winds have increased to near 85 mph (140 km/h) 
with higher gusts. Gradual strengthening is expected the next day or 
so.
 
Hurricane-force winds extend outward up to 30 miles (45 km) from the
center and tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 150 miles
(240 km).
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 981 mb (28.97 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
SURF:  Swells generated by Fausto are affecting portions of the west
coast of the Baja California Peninsula, and should persist for a
couple more days.  These swells are likely to cause life-threatening
surf and rip current conditions.  Please consult products from your
local weather office.
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 500 AM HST.
 
$$
Forecaster Kelly
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Forecast Advisory Number 14
item.description:
Issued at 0900 UTC WED JUL 22 2026 
000
WTPZ21 KNHC 220834
TCMEP1
 
HURRICANE FAUSTO FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  14
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026
0900 UTC WED JUL 22 2026
 
HURRICANE CENTER LOCATED NEAR 16.7N 120.7W AT 22/0900Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST-NORTHWEST OR 285 DEGREES AT   7 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE  981 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  75 KT WITH GUSTS TO  90 KT.
64 KT....... 25NE  10SE   0SW  15NW.
50 KT....... 50NE  60SE  20SW  30NW.
34 KT.......130NE 130SE 120SW  60NW.
4 M SEAS....300NE 180SE 120SW 210NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 16.7N 120.7W AT 22/0900Z
AT 22/0600Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 16.6N 120.4W
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1800Z 17.0N 121.9W
MAX WIND  80 KT...GUSTS 100 KT.
64 KT... 25NE  15SE   0SW  20NW.
50 KT... 60NE  50SE  30SW  40NW.
34 KT...130NE 130SE 110SW  80NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0600Z 17.4N 123.9W
MAX WIND  85 KT...GUSTS 105 KT.
64 KT... 30NE  20SE  15SW  25NW.
50 KT... 60NE  50SE  40SW  50NW.
34 KT...130NE 130SE 110SW  90NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1800Z 17.7N 126.4W
MAX WIND  80 KT...GUSTS 100 KT.
64 KT... 30NE  20SE  15SW  30NW.
50 KT... 60NE  50SE  40SW  50NW.
34 KT...140NE 130SE 110SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/0600Z 18.0N 129.1W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
64 KT... 30NE  20SE  15SW  30NW.
50 KT... 70NE  50SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...140NE 130SE 110SW 130NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 24/1800Z 18.3N 132.0W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
64 KT... 30NE  20SE  15SW  30NW.
50 KT... 70NE  50SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 120SE 100SW 130NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 25/0600Z 18.7N 134.7W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
64 KT... 30NE  15SE  15SW  20NW.
50 KT... 60NE  40SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 110SE 100SW 130NW.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 100 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 125 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 26/0600Z 19.3N 139.6W
MAX WIND  70 KT...GUSTS  85 KT.
50 KT... 50NE  30SE  30SW  50NW.
34 KT...130NE  90SE  80SW 120NW.
 
OUTLOOK VALID 27/0600Z 19.8N 144.6W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  80 KT.
50 KT... 40NE  20SE  20SW  40NW.
34 KT...110NE  80SE  60SW 110NW.
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 16.7N 120.7W
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 22/1500Z
 
$$
FORECASTER KELLY
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Forecast Discussion Number 14
item.description:
Issued at 1100 PM HST Tue Jul 21 2026 
000
WTPZ41 KNHC 220835
TCDEP1
 
Hurricane Fausto Discussion Number  14
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
1100 PM HST Tue Jul 21 2026
 
Hurricane Fausto's satellite presentation has improved throughout
the evening, with deep convection bursting over the low-level
center, with cold cloud tops near -80C, and a large curved band on
the southeast side of the system. Earlier SAR satellite imagery and
scatterometer data show that the center of the system was farther
east than anticipated. Satellite intensity estimates have generally
trended upward over the last several hours, with UW-CIMSS ADT and
AiDT at 74 and 65 kt, respectively. The latest TAFB subjective
intensity estimate was T5.0/90 kt. Given the improved satellite
presentation and these estimates, the initial intensity is raised to
75 kt for this advisory.
 
The system is moving west-northwestward at an estimated 285/7 kt
along the southern edge of a subtropical ridge to the north. The
ridge is forecast to strengthen and build westward over the next
several days, which should cause the hurricane to continue moving
west-northwestward to westward throughout the forecast period. The
latest NHC track forecast is near the previous one through 72 hours,
with little spread in the track guidance. Beyond 72 hours, the
forecast has once again shifted southward toward the latest HCCA and
GDMI consensus aids, and lies between these aids and the simple
consensus. Model spread increases toward the end of the forecast
period, with the GFS and some of the regional hurricane models
slower and farther north, while HCCA and GDMI are faster and farther
south.
 
Fausto is currently moving over warm ocean temperatures within an
environment of light to moderate wind shear and abundant mid-level
moisture, which should allow for further strengthening. Along the
forecast track, Fausto will pass over gradually cooling SSTs, and
will be moving roughly parallel to the 26C isotherm by 36 hours. At
the same time, mid-level relative humidity values are expected to
decrease through the remainder of the forecast period. The latest
NHC intensity forecast calls for continued strengthening though a
slightly earlier peak than previously forecast, followed by gradual
weakening. This forecast lies near the upper end of the guidance
envelope in the short term, before aligning closer to the consensus
as the system weakens. Should Fausto take a more northerly track,
the system would struggle to sustain deep convection, potentially
leading to a faster weakening trend than is currently forecast.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  22/0900Z 16.7N 120.7W   75 KT  85 MPH
 12H  22/1800Z 17.0N 121.9W   80 KT  90 MPH
 24H  23/0600Z 17.4N 123.9W   85 KT 100 MPH
 36H  23/1800Z 17.7N 126.4W   80 KT  90 MPH
 48H  24/0600Z 18.0N 129.1W   75 KT  85 MPH
 60H  24/1800Z 18.3N 132.0W   75 KT  85 MPH
 72H  25/0600Z 18.7N 134.7W   75 KT  85 MPH
 96H  26/0600Z 19.3N 139.6W   70 KT  80 MPH
120H  27/0600Z 19.8N 144.6W   65 KT  75 MPH
 
$$
Forecaster Kelly
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Hurricane Fausto Wind Speed Probabilities Number 14
item.description:
Issued at 0900 UTC WED JUL 22 2026 
000
FOPZ11 KNHC 220834
PWSEP1
                                                                    
HURRICANE FAUSTO WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  14                
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026               
0900 UTC WED JUL 22 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 0900Z THE CENTER OF HURRICANE FAUSTO WAS LOCATED NEAR LATITUDE   
16.7 NORTH...LONGITUDE 120.7 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS NEAR 
75 KTS...85 MPH...140 KM/H.                                         
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        06Z WED AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       06Z WED 18Z WED 06Z THU 18Z THU 06Z FRI 06Z SAT 06Z SUN
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             18Z WED 06Z THU 18Z THU 06Z FRI 06Z SAT 06Z SUN 06Z MON
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
15N 120W       34 89   X(89)   X(89)   X(89)   X(89)   X(89)   X(89)
 
15N 125W       34  X   3( 3)   3( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)   X( 6)
 
20N 125W       34  X   3( 3)  15(18)   X(18)   X(18)   X(18)   X(18)
 
15N 130W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   1( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
20N 130W       34  X   X( X)   1( 1)  26(27)  19(46)   X(46)   X(46)
20N 130W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)   4( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)
 
15N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   1( 3)   X( 3)
 
20N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  58(58)   8(66)   1(67)
20N 135W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  17(17)  10(27)   X(27)
20N 135W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   6( 6)   5(11)   X(11)
 
20N 140W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  56(57)  10(67)
20N 140W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  24(24)   8(32)
20N 140W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   9( 9)   5(14)
 
20N 145W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  45(46)
20N 145W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  15(15)
20N 145W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   5( 5)
 
20N 150W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER KELLY
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
